card.title: Transformer (Neural Network Architecture)
card.summary: A Transformer is a neural network architecture designed for sequence-to-sequence tasks that exclusively relies on self-attention mechanisms to weigh the importance of different parts of the input sequence, completely abandoning recurrent or convolutional layers.
card.how_it_works: Building on the core concept of the **Attention mechanism**, the Transformer processes entire sequences in parallel through stacked encoder and decoder blocks. Each block integrates multi-head self-attention to capture multi-faceted, long-range contextual relationships, followed by position-wise feed-forward **neural networks**, all stabilized by residual connections and layer normalization. Positional encodings are crucially injected to imbue the permutation-invariant attention with sequence order information, allowing the model to understand token positions without sequential processing.

key_components.name: Multi-Head Self-Attention
key_components.description: This mechanism simultaneously computes multiple attention weightings for each token, allowing the model to focus on different relational aspects within the input sequence, vastly improving contextual understanding compared to single-head attention.

key_components.name: Positional Encoding
key_components.description: These are vectors added to input embeddings that provide the model with crucial information about the absolute or relative position of each token, compensating for the permutation-invariance of attention.

key_components.name: Feed-Forward Networks (FFNs)
key_components.description: Applied independently and identically to each position, these are simple two-layer fully connected **neural networks** that non-linearly transform the attention-weighted representations at each token position.

key_components.name: Residual Connections
key_components.description: Also known as skip connections, these sum the input of a sub-layer with its output, mitigating the vanishing gradient problem and enabling the training of very deep Transformer models, much like in other deep **neural networks**.

key_components.name: Layer Normalization
key_components.description: This technique normalizes the inputs across the features for each sample independently, which significantly stabilizes and speeds up training by ensuring consistent activation scales throughout the network.

key_components.name: Encoder-Decoder Architecture
key_components.description: The standard structure comprises a stack of encoders that process the input sequence and a stack of decoders that generate the output sequence, with decoders utilizing cross-attention to attend to the encoder's final output.

use_cases: Translating natural language between different languages with high accuracy and fluency.
Generating coherent and contextually relevant text for chatbots, content creation, or code assistance.
Summarizing lengthy documents into concise overviews while preserving key information.
card.analogy: The Transformer is like an advanced **Attention mechanism** that has learned to parallel process entire documents at once, understanding the full context and relationships between all words simultaneously, rather than processing word-by-word like traditional recurrent **neural networks**.
